goal: Prepare for a technical job interview with mock questions, feedback on answers, and a day-by-day practice plan.
Optimize practice for short blocks (5/15/30 minutes).
Incorporate STAR-based behavioral interview prep: 6–8 core stories with bullet-point structure aiming for 90–120 seconds per answer.
Develop and practice a 30–60 second personal pitch template tailored to target roles.
constraints: Technical role; ready for both coding and behavioral parts.
Behavioral interview prep must use STAR framework.
Fintech startup context for tailoring questions and expectations.
Backend Python/Django experience; cloud services deployment and APIs (4 years).
Initial technical screen confirmed to be 45 minutes remote; potential on-site day if successful.
Timeline: 14 days until the remote screen.
Available practice windows: mornings before day job and early evenings.
decisions: Adopt short-block practice templates (5/15/30 minutes) as standard practice.
Focus each session on a single pattern or skill.
Maintain a simple one-line log per session for progress tracking.
Adopt STAR-based behavioral prep with 6–8 core stories mapped to competencies.
For each story, prepare 3–6 bullet points (Situation, Task, Actions, Result).
Target verbal delivery of 90–120 seconds per story.
Practice variations of prompts that map to the same story.
Include quick follow-ups and willingness to discuss technical details and trade-offs.
Include a 30–60s elevator summary for each story to handle brief prompts.
Add a personal pitch template (30–60 seconds) to the standard practice and include tailored variants.
Emphasize product impact and cross-functional ownership in examples.
Balance product and technical depth: start with impact-focused narrative and be prepared to drill into technical details if asked.
entities: micro drills: pattern drill, recovery drill, communication drill, edge-case blitz
one-line session log for progress tracking
core competency stories (ownership, conflict, impact, technical challenge, learning, trade-offs)
bullet-point STAR templates (3–6 bullets per story: Situation, Task, Actions, Result)
virtual interview readiness checklist (repo links, slides, terminal setup, etc.)
stress-increasing variations (time compression, multi-tasking, panel simulation)
recording, review & objective feedback rubric and metrics
solo mock prompts bank (interviewer prompts and interruptions)
ready-to-run 60-minute solo mock script with alarms and interruptions
alarm-driven timing checkpoints for practice sessions
product impact emphasis guidance (cross-functional ownership, measurable outcomes, risk awareness)
story mix guidance: aim for 6–8 total stories, mixing product and technical
database performance and SQL querying readiness
open: User time availability for daily blocks to tailor a 7-day micro-plan.
Provide two logistics items: interview date/time and daily time commitment.
Prepare a virtual interview readiness checklist (repo links, slides, terminal setup, etc.)
Interview format details: 45-minute remote screen confirmed; potential on-site day.
Location/time zone alignment: Central Europe (CET/CEST) to schedule blocks.
Exact interview date/time and daily time commitment to be confirmed in CET/CEST.
